summaryText: In part two of the End Times series, the pastor explores the character of the last days as described in scripture. He opens by observing the rapid technological and moral shifts in society, noting how they align with biblical prophecy. The message centers on three key developments: a rise in spiritual darkness, a rise in deception, and a rise in the battle for dominion over human hearts. The pastor explains that perilous times will be marked by self-love, moral collapse, and a rejection of sound doctrine in favor of teachings that suit personal desires. He warns of false gospels and the strategic deception aimed at believers, emphasizing the need for discernment rooted in the Holy Spirit. Despite the growing darkness, the message concludes with hope, pointing to the prophecy of God pouring out His Spirit on all flesh and the coming global revival. The call is for listeners to anchor themselves in the truth of Jesus, deny self, take up their cross, and live boldly for Christ in these prophetic times.
mainPoints: In the last days, there will be a rise in spiritual and moral darkness, characterized by self-love and the rejection of biblical truth.
Deception will intensify, with people rejecting sound doctrine for teachings that suit their feelings, leading to false gospels and a distortion of scripture.
A battle for dominion over human hearts is underway, setting the stage for the Antichrist, but God promises a counteracting outpouring of His Spirit and great revival.

versesMentioned.text: But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days. People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy, without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good, treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God— having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people.
When he arrived at the other side in the region of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men coming from the tombs met him. They were so violent that no one could pass that way.

For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine. Instead, to suit their own desires, they will gather around them a great number of teachers to say what their itching ears want to hear. They will turn their ears away from the truth and turn aside to myths.

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this," says the LORD Almighty, "and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
Then Jesus said to his disciples, "Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.
For false messiahs and false prophets will appear and perform great signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect.

Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ferocious wolves. By their fruit you will recognize them. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles? Likewise, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit. A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit. Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire. Thus, by their fruit you will recognize them.

It also forced all people, great and small, rich and poor, free and slave, to receive a mark on their right hands or on their foreheads, so that they could not buy or sell unless they had the mark, which is the name of the beast or the number of its name.
And afterward, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your old men will dream dreams, your young men will see visions.

Monday.scripture: 2 Timothy 3:1-5 (NIV) - 'But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days...'
Monday.focus: Recognizing the signs of spiritual darkness in the world.
Monday.action: Identify one area in culture or your own life where biblical values are being called 'wrong.' Pray for discernment.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see the spiritual climate around me. Help me to stand firm in Your truth and not be swayed by the world's values.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Timothy 4:3-4 (NIV) - 'For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine...'
Tuesday.focus: Guarding against deception and itching ears.
Tuesday.action: Evaluate one source of teaching you listen to (podcast, social media, etc.). Does it align with sound biblical doctrine?
Tuesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me discernment. Help me to love Your truth more than messages that simply make me feel good.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 16:24 (NIV) - 'Then Jesus said to his disciples, "Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves..."'
Wednesday.focus: The call to discipleship: denying self and taking up our cross.
Wednesday.action: Choose one personal desire or comfort to deny today as an act of surrender to Jesus.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to follow You today. Help me to deny my selfish desires and take up the cross of obedience, finding my life in You.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 7:15-20 (NIV) - 'Watch out for false prophets. By their fruit you will recognize them.'
Thursday.focus: Discerning truth by examining fruit, not just words.
Thursday.action: Reflect on the 'fruit' (character, impact, outcomes) in your own life. Does it point others to Christ?
Thursday.prayer: Father, prune my life so that I bear good fruit for Your kingdom. Let my life be a testament to Your truth and love.

Friday.scripture: Joel 2:28 (NIV) - 'And afterward, I will pour out my Spirit on all people...'
Friday.focus: The promise of God's Spirit and revival in the last days.
Friday.action: Pray specifically for one person you know who needs to experience the love and power of Jesus.
Friday.prayer: God of revival, pour out Your Spirit on my family, my community, and my nation. Use me as a vessel of Your hope and salvation.

Saturday.scripture: John 3:16 (NIV) - 'For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son...'
Saturday.focus: The foundation of our faith: God's love and salvation.
Saturday.action: Share the simple message of God's love in John 3:16 with someone, either in person or through a text/note.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, God, for Your incredible love. Give me courage and compassion to share the gospel with someone who needs to hear it this weekend.
